mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month. Higher policy rates reduce corporate borrowing capacity and lower present value of future earnings.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, labor market slack triggers a sharp contraction in consumer spending and corporate profit growth. This threshold breach signals an imminent economic slowdown that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in real sales of existing homes and a decline in the consumer price index indicates a healthy demand recovery without inflationary pressure. This joint signal confirms sustainable economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05), or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over individual series in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: During active monetary tightening cycles, a month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher borrowing costs. This supply resilience prevents earnings downgrades and generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during tightening periods, or the signal reverses direction (predicts negative returns) when FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25 percent.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 300 while the federal funds rate remains below 5.5 percent, the real policy rate turns deeply negative. This inflationary mismatch forces markets to price in aggressive future tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction signal predicts positive 20-day returns in over 60% of triggered cases, or the real rate differential shows no significant correlation with equity returns in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor market expansion that supports consumer income growth and corporate revenue. This positive labor trend directly translates into higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or null correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 consistently.

mechanism_narrative: When existing home sales fall below 1.6 million units, housing market weakness signals a broader contraction in consumer confidence and credit demand. This threshold breach triggers a risk-off sentiment that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances where HOUST drops below 1600, or the threshold signal loses predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: In the early stages of a post-recession expansion, a quarter-over-quarter increase in real GDP confirms that aggregate supply is recovering faster than expected. This positive growth surprise reduces recession fears and drives strong positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during post-recession periods, or the signal predicts negative returns when GDP growth exceeds 2.0 percent quarter-over-quarter.
